generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T OSTRYA. Ostrya Virginica, Willd. Natural order , Cupuliferæ. Common names , Iron-wood, Hop-hornbeam, Lever-wood. Preparation , Tincture of the heart-wood. Authorities. 1 , Dr. W. H. Burt, Trans. West. Inst. of Hom., 1867-8, p. 139, effects of decoction from heart of the tree evaporated to a powder and triturated to 2d dec., dose 20 grains; 1 a , same, second experiment with 20 grains of 3d dec. first day, 40 grains second day, 60 grains third and fourth days; 1 b , same, effects of 2 grains of crude powder; 1 c , same, effects of 5 grains of crude; 2 , E. H. King, Thesis Hahnemann Med. Coll., proving with 3d dil. in water, repeated doses; afterward tincture, doses of 5 to 100 drops; 2 a , same, took 1st dec. trit., 5 to 25-grain doses; 3 , same, proving of Dr. H. T. Snyder, with 50-drop doses of tincture; 4 , same, proving of Dr. W. S. Moffatt, proving with the tincture; 4 a , same, effects of large doses of fluid extract. HEAD. ► Light feeling in the head, with the distress in the epigastric and umbilical regions; walking aggravates the pain in the head (after two hours), [1] . ► Light feeling of the head, with the constant slimy taste (second day), [1a] . ► Dull headache, with a numb prickling feeling of the head, face, and hands, for the last hour; the head is especially affected (after three hours), [1b] . ► Dull headache, at 6 A.M. (second day), [3] . ► Slight headache in the morning, which continued most of the day, and in the evening had a sharp pain immediately back of the mastoid process of the temporal bone; the pain seemed to pass from within outwards (twenty-third day), [2] . ► Dull headache, with constant distress in the umbilicus (second day), [1a] . ► Slight headache, with an uneasy feeling throughout the system (half an hour after second dose, second day), [3] . ► Very slight headache, hardly sufficient to notice (twenty-fourth day), [2] . ► Slight pain in the head (after eleven hours, third day), [3] . [10.] ► By spells numb feeling of the head (after five hours), [1b] . ► Forehead. ► Severe headache in the frontal region; at first the headache was of a throbbing character, but gradually it grew more dull; worse on the right side (fifteen hours after first dose, twenty-second day), [2] . ► Walking or stooping produces severe throbbing pains in the forehead, passing off in an hour (second day), [1c] . ► Dull frontal headache, with frequent nausea, lasting but a moment (after two hours and a half), [1b] . ► Dull frontal headache (after fourteen hours), [1c] ; (third day), [3] . ► Slight frontal headache (after two hours), [1] ; (after ten hours), [1a] . ► Temple and Parietals. ► Severe pressing pain in the right temple; (fifteen minutes after first dose, second day), [3] . ► Pain in the right temple of a pressing character (fifth day), [3] . ► Dull pain in the temples (after five hours), [1a] . ► Slight pain in the right temple, and also in the right lower molars (fourth day), [3] . [20.] ► Dull headache, more in the right side of the head than in the left (after three-quarters of an hour, first day), [3] . ► Right lateral headache, in the morning (third day), [3] . EAR. ► Drawing pain deeply seated in the left ear (after first dose, second day), [3] . MOUTH. ► Boring pain in the right lower molar teeth (after first dose, second day), [3] . ► Slight pain in the right lower molars and also in the right temple (fourth day), [3] . ► *Tongue coated yellow at the root (fourth day), [3] . ► Slimy coppery taste in the mouth (after five hours and 2d and 4th dec.), [1a] . ► Slimy taste in the mouth (fourth day), [1a] . ► Constant slimy taste in the mouth, all day (third day), [1a] . ► Slight bitter taste in the mouth (fourth day), [3] . [30.] ► Sweet slimy taste, with distress in the umbilicus, at 2 P.M. (second day), [1c] . ► Rough bitter taste in the mouth (second day), [1c] . STOMACH. ► Appetite. ► Appetite very much increased, so that the prover can eat three times the usual quantity (after eleven hours, third day), [3] . ► Increase of appetite, [4] ; (twenty-first day), [2] . ► Awoke at 4 A.M., with great desire for something to eat (second day), [1a] . ► Slight increase of the appetite, [2a] . ► Decreased appetite (twenty-second day), [2] . ► *Loss of appetite for breakfast and dinner (second day), [1c] . ► Not much appetite, in the morning (twenty-second day), [2] . ► Nausea. ► *Frequent nausea, with the dull frontal headache (after two hours and a half), [1b] . [40.] ► Nausea, with the distress in stomach and bowels (third day), [1a] . ► Nausea (after a quarter of an hour, first day), [3] . ► Nausea, with the pains in the umbilicus (second day), [1a] . ► Nausea, with distress in the umbilicus (after one hour); severe (after three hours), [1a] . ► Nausea, with the distress in the epigastric and umbilical regions (first day), [1c] . ► Qualmishness, with the distress in stomach and bowels (after one hour), [1] . ► General Stomach. ► Stomach sour, with frequent eructations of food (after ten hours), [1b] . ► Awoke several times in the night with very sour stomach, with distress and soreness of the stomach and whole of the bowels (third night), [1a] . ► Sour stomach, in the evening (first day), [1a] . ► Distress in bowels and stomach, with qualmishness (after one hour), [1] . [50.] ► Distress in the stomach (after one hour), [1c] . ► Burning distress in the stomach (after fifteen minutes), [1] . ► Dull distress in the whole epigastrium (after one hour), [1b] . ► Uncomfortable sensation in the epigastric region, a sensation as if an indigestible substance were in the stomach, continuing until breakfast (7.30 A.M.), which partially relieved it; it totally disappeared before 12 M. (soon after first dose, sixteenth day); the same sensation as yesterday morning, but to a less extent, and lasting a shorter time (after first dose, seventeenth day), [2] . ► Rode all the forenoon in the country; the atmosphere was very cold; had frequent pain in the epigastric and umbilical regions, with nausea, several times; now have constant distress in the epigastric and umbilical regions (after five hours), [1c] . ► Constant distress in the epigastric and umbilical regions, with nausea, and light feeling in the head (after two hours), [1] . ► A number of times through the afternoon and evening had hard sickening pains in the epigastric and umbilical regions (first day), [1b] . ► Awoke several times in the night with cutting pains in the epigastric region (first night), [1c] . ► Sinking sensation in the epigastric region (after a quarter of an hour), [3] . ABDOMEN. ► Hypochondrium. ► Dull pains in the right hypochondrium, aggravated by walking, with the dull headache and nervousness (after five hours), [1b] . [60.] ► Have had constant dull pains in the right lobe of the liver and whole epigastrium for the last hour, with frequent eructations of sour fluid (after seven hours), [1b] . ► Umbilicus. ► Retraction of the umbilicus (after nine hours, third day), [3] . ► Several times through the day had hard pains in the umbilical region (third day), [1b] . ► Hard, sickening pains in the umbilicus at 3 P.M. (second day), [1b] . ► Frequent hard, cutting pains in the umbilicus, extending into the hypogastric region, with nausea (after four hours, second day); with desire for stool (after five hours, second day), [1a] . ► Neuralgic pain in the umbilical region (after nine hours, third day), [3] . ► Distress in the umbilicus with the nausea (after one hour); have had constant distress in umbilicus, with occasional pains in the stomach (after three hours). Have had constant distress in the umbilicus, with frequent sharp pains in same region and stomach (after five hours); constant and severe pain in umbilicus all the evening (first day); several times this morning have had a dull distress in the umbilicus (second day), [1a] . ► Hard, aching distress in the umbilical region, with severe nausea; the distress extends all over the bowels, but is the greatest in the umbilicus; this has been the leading symptom (second day), [1a] . ► Occasional pain in the umbilical region, with borborygmus and tenesmus (third day), [3] . ► General Abdomen. ► Rumbling in the bowels (after five hours); for the last hour loud rumbling of the bowels, with pains in the umbilical and hypogastric regions, and desire for stool, relieved with frequent emissions of very fetid gas from the bowels, showing that the liver is very prominently affected (after eleven hours, third day), [1a] . [70.] ► Some flatus (second night), [3] . ► Flatus, followed by a loose stool (eleven hours after first dose, second day), [3] . ► Have had frequent spells of distress in the abdomen, with rumbling in the bowels (after fourteen hours), [1c] . ► Cutting colicky pains in the abdomen, followed in a short time by a mushy stool, accompanied with burning at the anus and slight tenesmus, in the afternoon (twenty-fourth day), [2] . ► Awoke several times in the night with distress in the bowels, worse by moving (second night); occasional pains in the morning; constant distress in the whole of the bowels all day, with frequent nausea (third day); distress in the bowels, not so severe as yesterday (fourth day); for three days afterwards occasional pains through the bowels, [1a] . ► The bowels felt as if bound by lead poisoning (after nine hours, third day), [3] . RECTUM AND STOOL. ► The rectum feels sore; a protruded feeling after stool (fourth day), [3] . ► Desire for stool; natural stool, first one to-day (after three hours), [1a] . ► Slight inclination for stool, at 8 A.M. (fourth day), [3] . [80.] ► Loose stool (eleven hours after first dose, second day); with slight tenesmus, in the morning (third day); bilious stool with tenesmus and straining; afterwards (at noon) a bilious stool, very thin, with pressing for stool afterwards; in the evening a watery stool with tenesmus and sore feeling in the rectum (fourth day); loose stool without tenesmus (fifth day), [3] . ► Severe tenesmus (after seven hours), followed by diarrhic stool; tenesmus continued an hour and a half (third day), [3] . ► Mushy stool, accompanied with burning at the anus and slight tenesmus, in the afternoon (twenty-fourth day), [2] . ► Soft stool (after five hours, second day); dark-colored mushy, very fetid stool, with distress in the whole of the bowels (after two hours, fourth day), [1a] . ► Natural dark-colored stool (after fourteen hours), [1c] . ► Small natural stool (after two hours and a half); natural stool (after seven hours); black, dry, lumpy stool at 6 P.M. (second day); stool of natural consistence, but very black (third day), [1b] . BACK. ► Back so painful that it is with difficulty that I can turn over in bed (after thirteen hours); so painful that I cannot stand erect; it is a regular attack of lumbago, whether caused by the Ostryin or not, remains to be proved (after sixteen hours); the pains in the back remained four days, I took Macrotin, Bry. and Nux, to relieve it, the pain was so severe. A year ago I had an attack of lumbago which lasted a week, [1] . ► Dull pains in the whole dorsal region (after ten hours), [1b] . ► Have had a great deal of pain in the dorsal region, extending into the left hypochondriac region (third day), [1a] . ► 5 P.M. since 2, have had constant pain in the lumbar region, worse when stooping or walking (after seven hours), [1] . EXTREMITIES. [90.] ► Arms and legs ache (after five hours), [1b] . ► Dull pains in the arms and legs (after seven hours), [1b] . ► Fine drawing rheumatic pains in the first joint of the left forefinger and little finger, lasting ten minutes (after five hours), [1a] . ► Fine drawing pains in the fingers (second day), [1c] . ► Frequent drawing pains in the fingers in the morning (third day), [1a] . ► Awoke several times with a cramplike pain in the lower extremities (first night), [3] . ► Pain in the lower extremities, worse when walking (third day), [3] . ► Drawing pains in the right ankle (first day), [1c] . GENERALITIES. ► Very nervous, with dull headache, and dull pains in the right hypochondrium, aggravated by walking (after five hours), [1b] . ► Feeling nervous (first day), [1c] . [100.] ► Numb, prickling feeling of the head, face and hands, the head is especially affected, with the dull headache (after three hours), [1b] . ► Feeling very languid (after ten hours), [1b] . ► Feeling languid, with constant pain in the lumbar and sacral region (after eleven hours), [1] . ► Feeling of weakness or debility, with gentle perspiration covering the entire surface (after eleven hours, third day), [3] . ► Uneasy feeling throughout the system, with slight headache (half an hour after second dose, second day), [3] . ► Slept well, but feeling as though I had not slept any (second morning), [1b] . ► The system feels as though I had been acted upon by an active or drastic purgative (after eleven hours, third day), [3] . ► A slight tonic effect, [4a] . SLEEP. ► Constant disposition to yawn (after ten hours), [1b] . ► Rested and slept much better than the previous night (twenty-second day), [2] . [110.] ► Slept well and sound (twenty-third night), [2] . ► Slept well all night (third night), [3] . ► Did not sleep well, was very restless, as if produced by strong tea (fourth night), [3] . ► Slept but little, was not sleepy (twentieth night), [2] . FEVER. ► Cold, chilly sensations about the shoulders (second day), [1b] . ► Gentle perspiration covering the entire surface, with a feeling of weakness or debility (after eleven hours, third day), [3] . CONDITIONS. ► Aggravation. ► ( Morning ), 6 A.M., headache; right lateral headache; loss of appetite; dull distress in umbilicus; drawing pain in fingers. ► ( Afternoon ), Sickening pain in epigastrium; 3 P.M., pain at umbilicus; mushy stool with burning at the anus. ► ( Evening ), Sharp pain back of mastoid process of temple; sour stomach; sickening pain at epigastrium; pain in umbilicus. ► ( Night ), Cutting pain in epigastric region. ► ( Moving ), Distress in bowels. ► ( Stooping ), Throbbing pain in forehead; pain in lumbar region. ► ( Walking ), Throbbing pain in forehead; pain in head; pain in right hypochondrium; pain in lumbar region; pain in lower extremities. Clinical: Headache, dull; Head, numb; Intermittent fever; Liver, affections of; Lumbago; Malaria; Malarial anæmia

Drawing pain deep in l. ear. Arms and legs: aching; dull pains. Fine drawing rheumatic pains in l. fingers, morning. Awoke several times with cramp-like pain in lower limbs, walking. Drawing pains in r. ankle. Nervous, languid, weak, uneasy.

keynotes.text: The Hop-hornbeam, Ostrya virginica, is sometimes regarded as a variety of the common Hornbeam naturalised in America, though native to Southern Europe. The chief difference between them is that Ost. vulg. has pendulous and Ost. virg. upright catkins. The wood, from preparations of which the provings by Burt and others were made, is exceedingly hard and heavy. The symptoms show a marked action on the liver, with the usual dull headache and pain in back and shoulders; cutting, sinking pains in abdomen. Allen put in italics the following: "Tongue coated yellow at root." "Loss of appetite for breakfast and dinner." "Frequent nausea with the dull frontal headache." On the other hand there is increased appetite; hunger wakes the patient at 4 a.m.; and sinking at epigastrium. Sickening pains were much complained of. Some of the stools were very black. The symptoms were moving; stooping; walking. 6 a.m.; 4 a.m.; 3 p.m.

_raw: Antidoted by: Macro., Bry., Nux (lumbago). Compare: Waking in night hungry, Ign., Lyc. Black stools, Lept.

head.text: Light feeling in head (with stomach symptoms); walking. Dull headache with numb, prickly feeling in head, face, and hands; head. Sharp pain from within out back of mastoid process. Severe throbbing frontal headache, stooping. Dull frontal headache with nausea. Severe pressing pain r. temple; and r. molars. R. side headache, morning.

face.text: Moist freckles in face, head, and hands.

mouth_throat.text: Boring and sharp pain in r. lower molars. Tongue coated yellow at root. Taste: coppery, slimy; sweet, shiny; bitter; rough.

stomach_abdomen.text: Appetite greatly increased. Woke with hunger 4 a.m. Loss of appetite. Frequent nausea with the dull frontal headache. Sour stomach. Burning distress in epigastrium. Sensation as if indigestible substance in stomach; > by eating. Nausea and distress after riding in cold air. Hard sickening pains in epigastric and umbilical region; awaking him at night. Sinking in epigastrium. Dull pain in r. hypochondrium, by walking; with nervousness and dull headache. Dull pains in r. lobe of liver with sour eructations. Retraction of umbilicus. In umbilical region: frequent, hard sickening, cutting pains; extending into hypogastrium; with desire for stool; with borborygmi and tenesmus; neuralgic pain; constant distress. Rumbling; flatus; pain waking him in night. Bowels felt as if bound by lead poisoning.

stool_rectum.text: Rectum feels sore; protruded feeling after stool. Stool: loose; bilious with tenesmus and straining; loose without tenesmus; tenesmus followed by diarrhœic stool; mushy with burning at anus and tenesmus; small, natural; dark-coloured, natural; black, dry lumps 6 p.m.; very black.

back.text: Back so painful, can hardly turn in bed; cannot sit up (> by Macro., Bry., Nux). Dull pain in whole dorsal region; extending into l. hypochondrium. Constant lumbar and sacral pain, stooping or walking.

sleep.text: Constant desire to yawn. Sleep restless, as after strong tea.

fever_chill.text: Cold, chilly about shoulders. Gentle sweat over entire surface, with feeling of weakness. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main.

indications.text: The inner wood and bark of Ostrya virginica, Willdenow.Nat. Ord.—Cupuliferae.COMMON NAMES: Iron-wood, Hop-hornbeam, Lever-wood.

Botanical Source.—This is a small tree from 25 to 30 feet in height, remarkable for its fine, narrow, longitudinally divided, and brownish bark. The wood is white, hard, and strong. The leaves are oblong, ovate, subcordate, acuminate, unequally serrate, and somewhat downy; the buds acute. The sterile flowers are in cylindrical aments, scales orbicular-ovate, acuminate, ciliate, 1-flowered filaments somewhat united irregularly; anthers bearded at the summit. The fertile flowers are in pairs, numerous, in a short, oblong, pendulous, loosely imbricated, linear, terminal ament, with small, deciduous bracts; scales none, but each flower is inclosed in a membranous sac-like involucre, bristly hairy at the base, and which enlarges, forming a bladdery closed bag in fruit, these being imbricated to form a sort of strobile appearing like that of the hop. The ovary is 2-celled, 2-ovuled, crowned with entire and bearded border of the perianth, forming a small and seed-like, smooth nut. Styles 2, united at the base; nut lance-oblong, somewhat compressed, and included in the enlarged, imbricated, bladder-like sac (G.—W.).

History.—This is a tree common to the United States, growing in rich woods, and flowering in April and May. The flowers are green, and appear with the leaves, and the large and handsome oval-oblong strobiles are matured in August. The inner wood and bark are the parts used; they are bitter and yield their virtues to water. Prof. Trimble found it to contain 6.5 per cent of tannin referred to dried substance (Bull. of Pharm., 1895, p. 412).

Action, Medical Uses, and Dosage.—Iron-wood is antiperiodic, tonic, and alterative. It has been used with efficacy in intermittent fevers, neuralgic affections, dyspepsia, scrofula, and all diseases where an antiperiodic tonic is indicated. Dose of the decoction, 1 or 2 fluid ounces, 3 or 4 times a day; of the fluid extract, 1 fluid drachm.

Related Species.—Carpinus americana, Michaux. This is another tree known as Ironwood and Hornbeam, closely resembling the above. It grows from 10 to 20 feet high, has a smooth gray bark, with an irregularly ridged trunk, and very fine-grained, compact, white wood. The scales of the fertile aments are 3-parted, the middle segment being much the largest, oblique, with a lateral tooth, persistent, and becoming foliaceous. The nut small, ovoid, bony, ribbed, with a simple, one-sided, enlarged, and open leaf-like involucre. This tree is not bitter, and must not be confounded with the Ostrya (G.—W.).
King's American Dispensatory, 1898, was written by Harvey Wickes Felter, M.D., and John Uri Lloyd, Phr. M., Ph. D.
